title: Use the language server for renames, not find/replace
description: Did a project-wide find/replace to rename a function. It silently renamed an unrelated string in a markdown file and a comment in a test, neither of which I wanted touched.
context: Refactoring an exported function name across a TypeScript / Python / Go codebase.
resolution: Reverted, used the editor's 'Rename Symbol' (F2 in VS Code) which only touched bindings.
prevention: For renaming identifiers (functions, classes, variables, types): use the language server's rename action. Reserve plain find/replace for non-identifier text (strings, comments, docs).
trigger: About to do a find/replace whose pattern matches an identifier name
